EA030 — RTricks

EA030 — Environmental Analysis

RTricks — Statistical Computing Resources

Prof. Marc Los Huertos  •  Pomona College

Pomona College Environmental Analysis R / RMarkdown / knitr

Getting Started Basic Stats Advanced Stats │ Core R Skills Field & Lab Projects │ Resources

Welcome to the RTricks resource hub for EA030. Tutorials, worked examples, and reference materials for R in environmental data analysis — from loading your first dataset through mixed-effects models and spatial analysis. Each card links to a self-contained module. Cards marked coming soon are in progress.

Getting Started

Setup, orientation, and reproducible workflow fundamentals.

💻
R & RStudio Setup

Install R and RStudio, configure your environment, and verify with a test script.

coming soon 📁
Project Structure

Organise scripts, data, and outputs using RStudio Projects and a consistent folder hierarchy.

coming soon 🧶
RMarkdown & knitr

Weave prose, code, and output into reproducible reports. YAML headers, chunk options, rendering.

coming soon 🗃️
Data Import

Reading CSV, Excel, and field-data files. Handling encoding issues and common pitfalls.

coming soon

Basic Statistics

Descriptive summaries, inference, and foundational visualisation.

📊
Descriptive Statistics

Measures of centre and spread, frequency tables, base-R and dplyr summaries.

coming soon 📈
Data Visualisation

Core ggplot2 grammar: geoms, aesthetics, facets, themes, and export-ready figures.

coming soon 🔬
Hypothesis Testing

t-tests, Wilcoxon tests, proportions tests. Effect sizes, p-values, common misconceptions.

coming soon 📉
Simple Linear Regression

Fitting, diagnosing, and interpreting OLS models with lm(). Residual plots and leverage.

coming soon 🎲
ANOVA & Group Comparisons

One-way and two-way ANOVA, post-hoc tests, and visualising group differences.

coming soon 📐
Correlation Analysis

Pearson and Spearman correlation, scatterplot matrices, and interpreting correlation structure.

coming soon

Advanced Statistics

Multivariate methods, hierarchical models, and specialist tools for environmental data.

🌿
Multiple Regression & GLMs

Multiple predictors, interactions, model selection (AIC), and GLMs for count and binary data.

coming soon 🏔️
Mixed-Effects Models

Random intercepts and slopes with lme4. Nested and crossed random effects for field designs.

coming soon 🗺️
Spatial Analysis

Importing shapefiles, CRS transformations, spatial joins, and choropleth maps with sf.

coming soon 🕸️
Multivariate Ordination

PCA, NMDS, and RDA for community and environmental gradient data using vegan.

coming soon ⏱️
Time-Series Analysis

Decomposition, autocorrelation, ARIMA, and sensor-data workflows with lubridate and tsibble.

coming soon 🔁
Bootstrapping & Permutation

Resampling-based inference, confidence intervals, and randomisation tests.

coming soon

Core R Skills

General-purpose, transferable R techniques applicable beyond EA030.

🔧
Tidyverse Data Wrangling

dplyr verbs, tidyr pivoting, and pipe-based workflows for cleaning field and lab data.

coming soon 🐙
Git & GitHub for R

Version-control basics, committing from RStudio, branching, and submitting via pull request.

coming soon 📦
Package Management with renv

Lock package versions and share reproducible environments across machines.

coming soon 🔄
Functional Programming

purrr::map() and friends to replace loops, write cleaner code, and automate repetitive tasks.

coming soon 📋
Tables & Reporting

Publication-quality tables with kableExtra, gt, and flextable. Inline R in RMarkdown.

coming soon

EA030 Field & Lab Projects

Course-specific modules tied to EA030 assignments, field campaigns, and shared datasets.

🌱
Soil Carbon Analysis

Importing and cleaning soil survey data, fitting depth profiles, and comparing land-use treatments.

coming soon 💨
EPA PM2.5 Analysis

Analysing EPA particulate matter data for regional air quality trends.

project 💧
Water Quality Monitoring

Time-series of field sensor data, detecting exceedances, and visualising spatial variation.

coming soon 🦋
Biodiversity Surveys

Species richness, rarefaction curves, and community composition with vegan.

coming soon 🌡️
Climate Data & Trends

Downloading NOAA/EPA data, trend detection, and visualising temperature and precipitation records.

coming soon 🌦️
Regional Climate Trends

Analysing long-term temperature and precipitation patterns across Southern California using PRISM and NOAA station data. Trend detection with Mann-Kendall tests and visualisation of seasonal shifts.

climate 🏙️
Urban Heat Island

Raster data from Landsat, land-cover classification, and surface temperature analysis.

coming soon 📍
GPS & Field Data Integration

Joining GPS tracks with field observations, plotting on basemaps, and exporting for reports.

coming soon

Additional Resources

External references and community tools curated for EA030.

📖
R for Data Science (2e)

Hadley Wickham & Mine Cetinkaya-Rundel. The canonical tidyverse reference, freely online.

external 🔍
Quick-R Reference

Dense, searchable reference for base-R syntax — useful as a cheat-sheet during lab sessions.

external 🎨
ggplot2 Reference

Full function reference for every geom, stat, scale, and theme component in ggplot2.

external 📋
Posit Cheatsheets

Official one-page PDF cheatsheets for ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, RMarkdown, and more.

external

Quick Reference

Repository at github.com/marclos/RTricks. Each module contains an index.Rmd, a data/ folder, and rendered HTML. Clone or browse via the links.
